description_long: This picnic table features a rectangular wooden tabletop composed of four wide, dark-stained planks, supported by a robust frame of crossbeams and angled legs. Two long benches, each made from matching dark wood planks, are attached parallel to the longer sides of the tabletop, providing ample seating. The supporting structure beneath the tabletop consists of thick, sturdy beams arranged in an A-frame style, ensuring stability and durability. The wood has a smooth, slightly glossy finish, suggesting it is treated for outdoor use and weather resistance. The table and benches are integrated into a single, cohesive unit, with all visible fasteners and joints appearing solid and secure. There is no visible hole in the center of the tabletop for an umbrella, and the overall design is classic and functional, suitable for communal outdoor settings.
description: A dark wood picnic table with integrated benches and a sturdy A-frame support structure, finished with a smooth, slightly glossy surface. The design is classic and robust, ideal for outdoor communal use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the length of the tabletop and both benches, highlighting the width and symmetry of the seating arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the full length of one bench and the tabletop, with the supporting A-frame legs clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again showing the tabletop and both benches from the opposite end.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the other bench and the supporting structure beneath the table.
attribute_tabletop_support_structure: The tabletop is supported by thick wooden crossbeams and angled A-frame legs, which are securely connected to both the tabletop and the benches for maximum stability.
